tables.name: Market Whispers
rows: "The mask-sellers know your face before you buy one"
"Half the stalls here weren't here yesterday"
"Nobody pays in coin on the third night of the moon"
"The guild that runs this market has no name and no face"
"Ask about the empty stall at your own risk"
"Every mask here was worn by someone who didn't come back for it"

tables.name: Market Encounters
rows: A merchant who insists you've met before
A masked figure paying triple price for information about you
A city guard quietly buying a disguise
A child selling a mask far too fine for the stall it's on
A rival adventuring party, half-recognizable under masks
Someone wearing your own face, badly copied

sections.title: Setting
lines: The market only opens after dark, and only in certain alleys.
Buying a mask is a transaction; wearing it in the market is a promise.
The city watch pretends the market doesn't exist.

scene.title: A Face for Sale
scene.body: A mask-seller holds up a mask that looks unsettlingly like your own face and names a price far too low.
